name: 2000 Iranian Parliamentary Election (6th Majlis)
description: The February 2000 parliamentary election produced a landslide victory for the reformist coalition aligned with [President Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami). Reformist parties, led by the [Islamic Iran Participation Front](/orgs/islamic-iran-participation-front), won approximately 195 of 290 seats. It was the high-water mark of the reform movement: the Islamic Iran Participation Front's [Mohammad-Reza Khatami](/people/mohammad-reza-khatami) topped the Tehran ballot with nearly 1.8 million votes. The 6th [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly), led by [Speaker Mehdi Karroubi](/people/mehdi-karroubi), was the most reformist parliament in the Islamic Republic's history and was in constant conflict with the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council).
editorial_notes: Reformist landslide. Guardian Council subsequently blocked much of the 6th Majlis's legislation and then disqualified nearly all reformist candidates for the 2004 election.
